text:
In the quiet town of Willowleaf, there lived an owl named Oliver. He loved nothing more than counting nocturnal creatures. One sunny evening, Oliver decided to owl-watch at Rainbow Rock. Halfway up its smooth stone face seemed peculiar.

Underneath lay countless tiny figures. Do beetles keep secrets? Oliver thought.

He counted ninety. Surely, frogs. He tilted his head.

Then came bats. Maybe owls did owl-chat. He chuckled softly.

That night, Oliver dreamt of stars. Their twinkles whispered secrets.

Daymond Owlley found something shiny. A promise.

He'd Owl-chat.

Oooooh.

For friendship.

So he fluttered.

Next time. owl owl owl.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
